relations.note: Provides an explicit layout exception for data containers (charts/tables) away from the signature asymmetric diagonal corner pattern.

quotes: A rectangle with selectively enlarged opposite corner radii; the other two corners keep a slight radius.

large: 120px & 6px; banner: 48px & 6px; callout: 42px & 6px. Apply the large radius to one diagonal pair (e.g., top-left + bottom-right), the 6px radius to the other pair. Be consistent within an email

rules.intent: Maintain the signature modern, optimistic asymmetric corner motif consistently throughout design layouts.
rules.rule_text: Apply the asymmetric brand accent shape to containers housing callout text and photography: use selectively enlarged opposite corner radii for one diagonal pair (default top-left & bottom-right) and a slight radius on the other pair. In email (at 96dpi), the large pair uses a 120px radius (radius.general.large = 120px), banners use 48px (radius.general.banner = 48px), and small callouts use 42px (radius.general.callout = 42px). The remaining opposite pair always uses a 6px radius (radius.general.small = 6px). A consistent diagonal orientation must be maintained throughout a single email.

quotes: Exception: charts and tables use uniform rounded corners on all four corners (not the asymmetric accent shape). [GENERAL: 12px uniform radius.]

rules.intent: Differentiate structural charts and tables from illustrative callouts and photos by applying clean, uniform corners.
rules.rule_text: Charts and tables must use uniform rounded corners on all four corners instead of the asymmetric accent shape, using a 12px uniform radius (radius.infographic.panel = 12px).
